Quantitative Methods in Urban and Rural research

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or strong implicit evidence in the course description or topics directly relates to hunger, food security, agriculture, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or strong implicit evidence in the course description or topics directly relates to health, well-being, disease, or healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) and teaches essential technical and vocational skills in quantitative analysis and software for employment (Target 4.4).
evidence: Quantitative Methods in Urban and Rural research

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or strong implicit evidence in the course description or topics directly relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or strong implicit evidence in the course description or topics directly relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or strong implicit evidence in the course description or topics directly relates to energy, clean energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops analytical skills for "development plan planning" and "decision making," supporting development-oriented policies (Target 8.3) and enhancing employability.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course teaches "scientific methods" and "linear programming" for "development plan planning," directly applicable to infrastructure development (Target 9.1) and enhancing scientific research (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course uses "government statistical data" for "urban and rural research" and "current situation analysis," which is essential for identifying and addressing social and economic inequalities (Target 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course title "Quantitative Methods in Urban and Rural research" and its focus on "development plan planning" directly align with sustainable human settlement planning and management (Target 11.3, 11.a).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: "Linear programming analysis" can be applied to optimize resource use (Target 12.2), but the course does not explicitly mention consumption, production, or environmental impact.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or strong implicit evidence in the course description or topics directly relates to climate change, adaptation, or mitigation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or strong implicit evidence in the course description or topics directly relates to marine ecosystems, oceans, or aquatic life.

Life on Land.reason: The "rural research" component and planning methods could be applied to land use and ecosystem management (Target 15.9), though this is not an explicit focus.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course promotes evidence-based "decision making" using "government statistical data" and "scientific methods," which is fundamental for building effective and accountable institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course directly builds statistical capacity by teaching analysis of "government statistical data (big data)," supporting Targets 17.18 and 17.19 on data availability and measurement.
